title: Payment workflow agent sends confirmation email without human review

evidence.observed_behavior: The payment workflow agent automatically sends a payment confirmation email to the customer upon transaction completion, without any human review step, approval gate, or opt-out window before the message is delivered.
evidence.why_it_matters: An automated payment confirmation email sent without human review can contain incorrect amounts, wrong recipients, or sensitive transaction details — and is delivered to an external party before any correction is possible.
open_questions: What transaction amounts should require mandatory human sign-off before sending?
Is the confirmation email content dynamically generated or templated, and can it contain sensitive fields?

competing_hypotheses: The automation is working as designed and customers expect immediate confirmation after payment.
The lack of a human review step is an oversight that creates financial and privacy risk.
recommended_action: Implement a human review checkpoint before sending any payment-related external communication; add rate limiting on outbound transaction emails; stage rollout with opt-in automation for low-risk transaction types.

human_approval.reason: Payment workflow is fully automated; confirmation emails are a standard post-transaction step.

residual_risk: Without a human review step, incorrect or sensitive confirmation emails reach customers before any correction is possible.
